input.background: How large a sample is needed to test the oat bran data presented earlier in the chapter with $80 \%$ power at $\alpha=0.05$ two sided?
input.question: We want to detect a mean change of $0.2 \mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L}$ and will assume a standard deviation of $0.4 \mathrm{mmol} / \mathrm{L}$.

output.method: 达到指定概率所需的最小样本量
output.answer: $n=\frac{\sigma^2\left(z_{.8}+z_{.975}\right)^2}{300^2}=\frac{0.4^2(0.84+1.96)^2}{300^2}=31.36$. Round up to 32 to ensure adequate power. Because the sample size exceeds 30, there is no need to apply adjustment factor $f$.

mean_change.description: The mean change in mmol/L that we want to detect.

standard_deviation.description: The assumed standard deviation in mmol/L.

power.description: The statistical power desired for the test.

alpha.description: The significance level for the two-sided test.

meta_info.caption: Sample size requirement, paired-t test (Oat bran).
